  "opinion": "I've been using Facebook for years now, mostly to keep in touch with family and friends, but lately it's full of lies and nonsense. As a man who has spent much of his life cleaning up messes, both literally and figuratively, I know all too well that you have to be careful about what lands on your screen. It reminds me of the times when I'd find dirt or grime in places where no one wants it - you just ignore it if possible, but sometimes you've got to clean it off properly.\n\nLooking at my family background, my father went to university and knew a bit more about life than my mother who didn't have much schooling. But that doesn't help me with social media any more than knowing I'm an atheist does - no one's perfect anyway. With all this fake stuff around, I think the best thing is just not to believe everything you see online.\n\nI spend several hours every day on these platforms because they're convenient for staying connected without having to travel far or do much planning myself. But it gets frustrating when people share half-truths and outright lies as if they were fact. It's like dealing with someone who doesn't know how to properly clean - you end up having mess everywhere.\n\nI'm not sure what the ideal solution would be, but perhaps we need more people checking their information before posting it? That sounds like hard work though, which is probably why my ocean scores suggest I'm a low conscientiousness type. But for dealing with fake content personally, I just block those who bother me and try to remember that most of the stuff online isn't worth getting worked up about.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
I've been using Facebook for years now, mostly to keep in touch with family and friends, but lately it's full of lies and nonsense. As a man who has spent much of his life cleaning up messes, both literally and figuratively, I know all too well that you have to be careful about what lands on your screen. It reminds me of the times when I'd find dirt or grime in places where no one wants it - you just ignore it if possible, but sometimes you've got to clean it off properly.

Looking at my family background, my father went to university and knew a bit more about life than my mother who didn't have much schooling. But that doesn't help me with social media any more than knowing I'm an atheist does - no one's perfect anyway. With all this fake stuff around, I think the best thing is just not to believe everything you see online.

I spend several hours every day on these platforms because they're convenient for staying connected without having to travel far or do much planning myself. But it gets frustrating when people share half-truths and outright lies as if they were fact. It's like dealing with someone who doesn't know how to properly clean - you end up having mess everywhere.

I'm not sure what the ideal solution would be, but perhaps we need more people checking their information before posting it? That sounds like hard work though, which is probably why my ocean scores suggest I'm a low conscientiousness type. But for dealing with fake content personally, I just block those who bother me and try to remember that most of the stuff online isn't worth getting worked up about.
